title: Archivists, conservators and curators
description: Appraise, edit, and direct safekeeping of permanent records and historically valuable documents. Participate in research activities based on archival materials. Administer collections, such as artwork, collectibles, historic items, or scientific specimens of museums or other institutions. May conduct instructional, research, or public service activities of institution. Restore, maintain, or prepare objects in museum collections for storage, research, or exhibit. May work with specimens such as fossils, skeletal parts, or botanicals; or artifacts, textiles, or art. May identify and record objects or install and arrange them in exhibits. Includes book or document conservators. (Based on 10 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Research and record the origins and historical significance of archival materials, and provide information from the institution's holdings to other curators and the public.
Evaluate services received on the tour, and report findings to tour organizers.
Compile and maintain records relating to circulation, materials, and equipment in libraries or archival collections.
Plan and conduct special research projects in areas of interest or expertise to further the understanding and preservation of collections.
Organize archival records and develop classification systems to facilitate access to archival materials, and create and maintain accessible, retrievable computer archives and databases.
Authenticate and appraise historical documents and archival materials, and advise on preservation procedures, including temperature and humidity control.
Prepare archival records, such as document descriptions, to allow easy access to information, and provide reference services and assistance to users needing archival materials.
Repair, restore, and reassemble artifacts, designing and fabricating missing or broken parts as necessary to maintain their origin and appearance.
Conduct reference searches, using printed materials and in-house and online databases, to assist patrons in finding and using library or archival resources.
Plan and organize the acquisition, storage, and exhibition of collections, including the selection of exhibition themes and designs, and develop or install exhibit materials.
Train and supervise curatorial, fiscal, technical, research, and clerical staff, as well as volunteers or interns, ensuring proper handling and care of collections.
Photograph objects for documentation and prepare artifacts for storage and shipping, ensuring their safety and condition.
Compile data and create statistical reports on library usage to analyse and improve service.
Attend meetings, conventions, and civic events to promote the institution's services and maintain community alliances.
Develop and maintain an institution's registration, cataloging, and record-keeping systems using computer databases.
Inform workers about the interests or special needs of specific groups and ensure quality service and compliance with specifications through observation and evaluation of workers' performance.
Inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established standards such as cleanliness and maintenance, and assess safety and defects.
Meet with managers or other supervisors to stay informed of operational changes and collaborate with staff to plan or develop programs or schedules of activities.
Resolve customer complaints regarding worker performance or services rendered and apply customer feedback to service improvement efforts.
Recruit, hire, train staff members in proper operational procedures, and assign work schedules to ensure quality and timely delivery of service.
Take disciplinary action to address performance problems and recommend measures to improve worker motivation and work methods.
